name: Amr ibn Qaraza
title: Imam's Shield
shortDescription: An envoy who carried messages between the Imam and Ibn Sa'd, and on Ashura shielded the Imam from arrows with his body.
fullStory:
Amr ibn Qaraza al-Ansari was a sincere and renowned Shia from Kufa. He joined the Imam a few days before Ashura and acted as one of the Imam's envoys, carrying messages to Umar ibn Sa'd.

Interestingly, his brother (Ali ibn Qaraza) was in Ibn Sa'd's army. On Ashura, Amr bravely went to the battlefield and, after a visible fight, returned to the Imam.

Amr showed the pinnacle of devotion when he stood in front of Imam Hussain (AS) and deflected every arrow aimed at the Imam with his hands and chest so the Imam wouldn't be harmed.

He took so many arrows that his body resembled a hedgehog. In his final moments, while covered in blood, he said to the Imam: 'O son of the Prophet! Have I fulfilled my covenant?' The Imam replied: 'Yes, you will be in paradise ahead of me.'
quoteArabic: يا ابْنَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ، هَلْ وَفَيْتُ؟...
quote: (His last words to the Imam:) O son of the Messenger of God! Have I fulfilled my covenant?
